programme.title: Dave Brubeck Trio, Cannonball Adderley Quintet, Charles Mingus Sextet & Jimmy Smith
programme.sub-title: Smith, Adderley, Brubeck, Mingus - Newport Jazz F.
programme.desc: The Newport Jazz Festival, first established in the North-American town of Newport, Rhode Island in the summer of 1954, has now grown to become one of the largest multi-day celebrations of jazz worldwide. It has resulted in numerous famous live albums from top-rate jazz stars, and has spawned several worldwide tours, including editions of the Newport Jazz Festival in Rotterdam, The Netherlands. In October 1972, an outstanding lineup played shoulder-to-shoulder at the 7th Newport Jazz Festival held in Rotterdam's then-recently built concert venue De Doelen. These four masterful groups consisted of alto saxophonist "Cannonball" Adderley and his quintet (Nat Adderley on cornet, George Duke on keyboards, Walter Booker on bass, and Roy McCurdy on drums), Hammond organist Jimmy Smith and his band (Art Farmer on flugelhorn, Illinois Jacquet on tenor saxophone, Kenny Burrell on guitar, Clark Terry on trumpet, and Roy Haynes on drums), pianist Dave Brubeck and his trio (Jack Six on bass, Alan Dawson on drums) with accompaniment from Paul Desmond on alto saxophone and Gerry Mulligan on baritone saxophone, and bassist Charles Mingus and his group (Joe Gardner on trumpet, Hamiet Bluiett on baritone saxophone, John Foster on piano, Roy Brooks on drums) with the great high-note trumpeter Cat Anderson as a guest.

programme.title: Milt Jackson
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Freddie Hubbard, Al Grey, Alvin Batiste & Al Casey
programme.sub-title: Freddie Hubbard & The Satchmo Legacy Live in 1987
programme.desc: Since 1977, Estival is a summer jazz festival in Switzerland, Lugano. Estival offers a thrilling and particularly surprising line-up that explores the rich world of contemporary music whilst promoting the understanding of different cultures, tolerance, and co-existence. This concert of Freddie Hubbard and the Satchmo Legacy in 1987, highlights the bebop legend's classic style and silky tone. Hubbard is an American jazz trumpeter whose unmistakable and influential tone contributed to new perspectives for modern jazz and bebop. He was also a well-known part of Art Blakey and the Jazz Messengers and is primarily known for playing bebop, hard bop, and post-bop styles from the early '60s onwards.

programme.title: Sarah Vaughan, Richard Davis, Ronnell Bright & Art Morgan
programme.desc: Sarah Vaughan: Live in '58 & 64 features the Divine One in her prime, wrapping her sultry voice around jazz standards such as “Lover Man”, “Misty”, and “I Got Rhythm” and popular showtunes such as “Over The Rainbow” and “Maria”. One of the greatest voices of the 20th Century, her renditions of songs by Harold Arlen, Leonard Bernstein, Johnny Burke, the Gershwins, and Stephen Sondheim are pure diva magic. These three performances demonstrate why Sarah Vaughan is invariably mentioned in the same breath as Ella Fitzgerald and Billie Holiday.

programme.title: Bill Evans
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Charles Mingus, Jaki Byard, Dannie Richmond & Johnny Coles
programme.sub-title: Charles Mingus And Eric Dolphy live in Liège
programme.desc: Charles Mingus showcases an exceptional concert performed in April 1965 featuring his most celebrated lineup: Jaki Byard (piano), Dannie Richmond (drums), Johnny Coles (trumpet), Clifford Jordan (tenor sax) and the great Eric Dolphy (alto sax, flute and bass clarinet). Recorded within an eight-day span, less than three months before Dolphy's death, the three concerts showcase Mingus's visionary leadership and the band's incredible depth and diversity with unique performances and arrangements of classics including ‘So Long Eric' and the groundbreaking ‘Meditations On Integration'.

programme.title: Miles Davis
programme.desc: American trumpeter Miles Davis (1926-1991) was a pioneering jazz musician from the late 1940s to the late 1980s. His influence on 20th century music is hard to overstate, as he trailblazed bebop, cool jazz, hardbop, modal jazz, and electronic music. On October 11, 1964, Miles Davis performed at Teatro Dell'Arte in Milan. With tenor saxophonist Wayne Shorter, pianist Herbie Hancock, bassist Ron Carter and drummer Tony Williams, Davis performed Autumn Leaves, My Funny Valentine, All Blues, All of You, and Joshua.

programme.title: Bootsy Collins
programme.sub-title: Casper the Funky Ghost: Bootsy Collins à Vienne
programme.desc: Bootsy Collins, famed for playing with George Clinton and his very own Rubber Band, is used to playing syncopated, hard and relentlessly rhythmic music. With this unique style Bootsy was a major influence on the development of funk. Now in his 60s, the bassist seems animated by a new energy that reflects his new work on the album ‘Tha Funk Capitol of the World'. With this album, Bootsy Collins pays tribute to the artists with whom he worked during his frantic career: from James Brown and Parliament-Funkadelic to Fatboy Slim. During this concert, recorded at the festival Jazz à Vienne, Bootsy maintains that same energy, giving a spectacular show.

programme.title: Václav Luks & Collegium 1704
programme.desc: J. S. Bach's six Brandenburg Concerto's belong to his best-known works. The composer wrote these concertos between 1711 and 1720 and dedicated them in 1721 to Christian Ludwig, Margrave of Brandenburg. In celebration of the pieces' 300th anniversary, Czech harpsichordist and conductor Václav Luks and the renowned Baroque ensemble Collegium 1704 recorded all six Brandenburg Concertos on historical instruments in 2021. The concertos are based on the Italian concerto grosso form, in which a group of solo instruments is set against a large ensemble. Bach's Brandenburg Concertos feature remarkable combinations of solo instruments and virtuoso solos. This performance was recorded at the Hall of Mirrors in the Köthen Castle, Germany.

programme.title: Herbert Blomstedt & Gewandhausorchester Leipzig
programme.sub-title: Symphonies in D by Mozart and Voríšek
programme.desc: Conductor Herbert Blomstedt and the Gewandhausorchester take us on a musical journey to the Czech Republic in this 2020 concert from Leipzig's Gewandhaus. The program opens with Bohemian composer Jan Václav Hugo Voríšek's Symphony in D major, Op. 23 (1821). Although the composition – Voríšek's only symphony – was never performed during the composer's lifetime, it has become one of his most-performed works today. Also on the program is Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart's Symphony No. 38 in D major, K. 504. This work, which premiered while Mozart was visiting Prague in 1787, is often referred to as the ‘Prague Symphony'.

programme.title: Seong-Jin Cho
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Ivan Fischer, Budapest Festival Orchestra & Richard Goode
programme.sub-title: Beethoven - Symphonies and Piano Concerto No. 4
programme.desc: Iván Fischer leads his Budapest Festival Orchestra (BFO) in a concert program dedicated to the music of Ludwig van Beethoven. The program opens with the composer's Symphony No. 1 in C major, Op. 21. Afterward, Fischer conducts Beethoven's Piano Concerto No. 4 in G major, Op. 58, in which Richard Goode features as the soloist. The acclaimed American pianist (1943) is known for his interpretations of the Beethoven repertoire. Goode recorded all five Beethoven piano concertos with the BFO and Fischer, earning him exceptional critical acclaim and a Grammy Award nomination. As an encore, Goode performs the Sarabande from J. S. Bach's Partita in B-flat major, BWV 825. The program closes with Beethoven's famous Symphony No. 5 in C minor, Op. 67. This performance was recorded at the Béla Bartók National Concert Hall in Budapest, Hungary, on January 29, 2017.

programme.title: Les Dissonances
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Mademoiselle Karen, Jakob Munch, Danish National Symphony Orchestra & Emma Smith
programme.desc: One night only! Welcome to the spectacular and decadent world of The Babylon Hotel, where music pours out of every crevice like bubbling champagne. In this concert, the upper class meets the underworld in a melting pot of euphoria and extravagance, nostalgia and pleasure with a carefree sinfulness only seen in the exuberant nightlife of the 1920s around the world. The concert features iconic music from movies and series, such as The Great Gatsby, Burlesque, and Babylon Berlin. The music is performed by the Danish National Symphony Orchestra and DR Big Band under the direction of Miho Hazama. The concert also features the Moka Efti Orchestra, and singers Madame le Pustra, Emma Smith, Mademoiselle Karen, Jakob Munch, and Nikko Weidemann, as well as dancers of Sweet Burlesque. This performance was recorded at the DR Koncerthuset in Copenhagen, Denmark, in May 2023.

programme.title: Psychedelic Rock
programme.desc: The sound of psychedelic rock involved electronic instruments, guitar effects, oriental instruments, unconventional song structures, lyrics that referenced drugs or fantasy books, with extended instrumental sections. The genre bridged the transition from early blues and folk-based rock to progressive rock and hard rock. This episode features The Moody Blues, Emerson Lake & Palmer and Traffic

programme.title: Jazz Rock
programme.desc: Jazz rock generally grew out of the artistically ambitious rock subgenres of the late 1960s and early 1970s psychedelia, progressive rock, and the singer-songwriter movement. Jazz rock includes extensive improvisation and features soloists with distinctive sounds. This programme explores the music of artists Steely Dan, Doobie Brothers, Boz Scaggs.

programme.title: ABBA
programme.desc: ABBA's was an extraordinary journey through the pages of music history – it took them from throwaway Eurovision turn to highly respected pop icons. This programme provides a perfect career retrospective it looks beyond the glitz and the glamour to get to the heart of what made ABBA so special. Also included is superb footage of the band in action performing their greatest hits. Featuring highlights from: Waterloo, Mamma Mia, Knowing Me, Knowing You, Dancing Queen, Thank You For The Music, and more...

programme.title: Amor A La Moda
programme.desc: Luego de años manejando la moda en la ciudad, Lauren decide mudarse a casa. A cambio de sus servicios de organización, Rob, el dueño de una compañía de mudanzas, se propone ayudarla a admirar la belleza de Seattle. Protagonizan Kat Graham y Kendrick Sampson

programme.title: Una detective aficionada
programme.desc: Una escritora de viajes es testigo de un asesinato en un hotel de Malta y queda atrapada en una red de intriga y espionaje mientras intenta encontrar al asesino antes de que el asesino la encuentre a ella. Protagonizada por Nathalie Kelley, Colin Donnell y Patti Murin.

programme.title: Por Siempre Juntos
programme.desc: Durante mucho tiempo, Ellie Hemway ha planeando minuciosamente la boda de su hermana. Cuando los invitados llegan a una playa paradisíaca, se encuentra con su exnovio, Greg, que también es el padrino.

programme.title: Navidad en el Hotel Plaza
programme.desc: Cuando la historiadora Jessica es contratada para crear la exhibición navideña de "El Plaza", descubre hechos importantes mientras trabaja en equipo con el guapo decorador Nick para darle vida a la exhibición. Protagonizada por Elizabeth Henstridge y Ryan Paevey.

programme.title: Los Misterios de Jane: La Herencia Desaparecida
programme.desc: La cantante Jane DaSilva hereda la fundación familiar que busca justicia para quienes no pueden valerse por sí mismos. Junto con el detective John Cameron, aporta esperanza a muchos casos desesperados. Protagonizada por Jodie Sweetin y Stephen Huszar.

programme.title: That Dirty Black Bag
programme.desc: La historia del enfrentamiento entre dos hombres: McCoy, un sheriff aparentemente incorruptible con un oscuro pasado, y Red Bill, un sucio y taciturno asesino motivado por la venganza.

programme.title: Dark Winds
programme.desc: El policía tribal navajo Joe Leaphorn debe resolver crímenes junto a su nuevo ayudante, Jim Chee, que vuelve a la reserva por primera vez desde que era niño. Ellos se enfrentarán a fuerzas malignas, terrenales y sobrenaturales.

programme.title: Dark Winds
programme.desc: Leaphorn y Chee entrevistan al testigo superviviente de los asesinatos del motel Big Rock. Chee se reúne con el forense. La causa de la muerte sigue siendo un misterio. Manuelito realiza un descubrimiento escalofriante.

programme.title: Dark Winds
programme.desc: La sobrina de Leaphorn celebra su Kinaaldá mientras Leaphorn recupera los cuerpos de las víctimas de asesinato. Manuelito y Chee investigan el paradero de la familia mormona desaparecida.

programme.title: Dark Winds
programme.desc: Leaphorn sospecha que todos los crímenes ocurridos en la reserva están relacionados. Leaphorn invita a cenar a Chee. Manuelito realiza un descubrimiento.

programme.title: Dark Winds
programme.desc: Mientras custodia a un sospechoso relevante, Leaphorn descubre que detrás de este crimen hay más de lo que hubiera imaginado. Manuelito se enfrenta a sus peores temores. Chee debe elegir entre su carrera y su pueblo.

programme.title: El señor de los cielos: Ximena
programme.desc: Ximena acusa a Aurelio de haber mandado a matar a Santiago, y él le ruega no decirle nada a sus hijas. Rutila la ve morir y le dice a su padre que lo hará pagar por esa muerte.

programme.title: La patrona
programme.desc: Ricardo y Patricia se acercan, la pasión entre ellos crece cada vez más. Verónica se presenta ante los lobos como la nueva inversionista y le explica a Antonia que su esposo tenía minas.

programme.title: La patrona
programme.desc: Constanza y su hijo tienen un emotivo encuentro en la Fortaleza. Antonia acepta la sociedad con Verónica. Aníbal sabe que su hijo le declaró la guerra. El juez Villegas conoce a una sexóloga.

programme.title: La patrona
programme.desc: Gertrudis negocia con Verónica. Patricia le confiesa a Ricardo que él y Alejandro son hermanos. Irene se queda boquiabierta con la revelación. Aníbal teme que puedan acabar con su hijo.

programme.title: Al otro lado del muro
programme.sub-title: Sofía es blanco de un atentado
programme.desc: El gobernador impide que Sofía escape. En los alrededores del hotel, los hombres de don Chente le tienden una emboscada a la primera dama. Eliza y los niños sufren un disgusto como inmigrantes.

programme.title: Al otro lado del muro
programme.desc: El gobernador descubre que Sofía tiene un amante. Un video de las cámaras de seguridad lo deja mudo. Pastrana le pisa los talones a Joel, a Sofía y a su hija en su escape a la frontera.

programme.title: Sin senos sí hay paraíso
programme.desc: Gato Gordo secuestra a Catalina para operarle los senos. Martina la duerme con una pastilla para poder hacerle la cirugía. ¿Lo conseguirá? Daniela visita a su madre en el hospital.

programme.title: Sin senos sí hay paraíso
programme.desc: Daniela está como loca sin saber dónde ni quién la secuestró, hasta que Marcial la visita. La hija de la Diabla no descansa en su objetivo, quiere como sea, que Nachito la haga suya.

programme.title: Corazón valiente
programme.desc: Fernanda y Juan Marcos hacen el amor. Ella le confiesa a su padre que intentó matarlo. Miguel le pide a Nora ayuda para destruir a Bernardo. Samantha y Nicolás sufren un atentado.

programme.title: Corazón valiente
programme.desc: Samantha se muda con Nicolás para protegerlo. Ella y Willy se hacen la prueba de ADN, pero Fernanda logra cambiar los resultados. Nora insiste en su inocencia y accede a hablar de su pasado.

programme.title: Corazón valiente
programme.desc: Se incendia la cárcel y la vida de Ángela corre peligro. Bernardo cita a Esteban. Miguel tiene un encuentro romántico con Cecilia. Juan Marcos sigue sin querer saber nada de Ángela, ni de sus confesiones tardías.

programme.title: Corazón valiente
programme.desc: Ángela y Nora no aparecen tras el incendio. Willy saca a Samantha de la casa de Nicolás. Él y Fernanda descubren que Nora es su madre. El Vikingo le dice a Juan Marcos que Luis lo odiaba y que Ángela era su cómplice.

programme.title: Corazón valiente
programme.desc: Juan Marcos se entera por una de las celadoras de que Ángela es inocente. Las pruebas de ADN revelan que Samantha y Willy no son hermanos. Juan Marcos accede a casarse con Fernanda, pero reciben una llamada impactante.

programme.title: Corazón valiente
programme.desc: Fernanda no puede disimular su frustración al saber que Ángela está viva. Juan Pablo descubre que no se trata de Ángela. Lorena cambió la prueba de ADN de Samantha y Willy. Ramos sufre un infarto.

programme.title: Corazón valiente
programme.desc: Samantha le pide el divorcio a Willy, está en shock al pensar que son hermanos. Esteban le declara su amor y ella decide quedarse con su hijo. Vicente amenaza de muerte a Fernanda.

programme.title: Corazón valiente
programme.desc: Esteban besa a Samantha y ella no sabe qué hacer. Esteban le dice a Willy que la ama, que debe resignarse porque ya la perdió. Se llevan preso a Miguel porque Bernardo le sembró droga en la moto.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: This show explores a region you don't normally associate with smoking: Asia. From Chinese char siu pork to smoky tangerine flan, learn to create a unique fusion of Asian barbecue and American wood smoke. (S2 E03)

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: Begin Season 2 with a refresher course on the basic smoking techniques and smokers, from rotisserie-smoking chicken to smoke-roasting stuffed pork loin. Learn how to smoke on a kettle grill, pellet smoker, electric smoker, and offset barrel smoker. (S2 E01)

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: Ribs epitomize barbecue, combining well-marbled, rich-tasting meat with the primal pleasure of eating with your bare hands. Embark on a rib world tour from St. Louis to Chinatown. Plus, learn how to build an onion bomb that explodes with flavor. (S2 E04)

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: Some of the world's greatest poultry dishes owe their soulful character to wood smoke. Find out how to master the fundamentals of smoking poultry—from curing and brining to smoking in charcoal smokers and pellet grills. Plus, discover how to electrify barbecue sauce with chipotle (smoked jalapeño) chilies. (S2 E02)

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: Smoke is the soul of barbecue. In this episode, learn to use an electric smoker, offset smoker, and upright barrel smoker to make delicious smoked treats. On the menu: cold smoked scallops with smoked tomatoes and jicama mango salsa smoke-roasted chicken with horseradish dip and pecan-smoked baby back ribs with prickly pear barbecue sauce. (S1 E01)

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: In this episode, learn how to make luscious barbecued Berkshire pork belly house-cured pastrami and Kentucky-style barbecued lamb shoulder with an unusual black barbecue sauce called "dip". Plus, check out a new take on a classic cocktail–a smoked Manhattan! (S1 E02)

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: It's surf and turf on Project Smoke! Learn to use smoke to jazz up this classic combo with smoked shrimp cocktail fired up with chipotle chiles and a Yucatan orange cocktail sauce fresh bacon-wrapped, lemon-and-dill-stuffed trout smoked on cedar planks Santa Maria tri-tip reverse-seared on a pellet grill and Chinese tea-smoked duck served taco style. (S1 E03)

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: Get an inside look at the tools and fuels you need to get creative with ribs! Learn to make gigantic, grass-fed, barbecued beef ribs Jamaican jerk spareribs from heritage breed Berkshire hogs smoked over exotic pimento wood and spice-smoked Colorado lamb ribs with cherry barbecue sauce. Plus delectably different sides: apple-enriched, hickory-smoked coleslaw and bacon-cheddar cheese cornbread. (S1 E04)

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: This show is for people who want to smoke, but don't have access to a conventional wood- or charcoal-burning smoker. Learn how to use unconventional methods indoors to smoke a wide variety of ingredients from eggplant to salmon. Plus, how to smoke authentic Jamaican jerk chicken using your gas grill. (S1 E05)

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: This show celebrates beef in all its smoky glory, from a new twist on beef jerky to beef tenderloin smoked in an upright barrel smoker and Texas-style smoked brisket. Plus, learn how to make smokehouse beans and ember-roasted bell peppers. (S1 E06)

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: It's Bacon Mania on Project Smoke! Learn to make bacon from scratch at home and a new twist on Canadian bacon: pork loin in fennel brine smoked in a ceramic cooker. Then check out the "Tulsa Torpedo", a spectacular three pounds of porky goodness encased in a crispy bacon weave. Plus, bacon surf and turf in the form of bacon-wrapped Snow Crab Poppers. (S1 E07)

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: Go hog wild with this pork-themed episode! Learn to use a wood-burning rotisserie to make a coffee-crusted whole pork loin with redeye barbecue sauce. Then find out how to make herbed pork shoulder with peppery West Virginia mustard sauce. Plus, find out how to cure and hickory-smoke a spectacular shoulder ham and get the scoop on how to make barbecued cabbage with smoked cheese and chorizo. (S1 E08)

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: Don't wait until Thanksgiving to try these new twists on holiday favorites: whiskey-brined turkey smoked over maple wood bacon-stuffed barbecued onions smoky creamed corn fiery jalapeno cranberry salsa and a smoky bacon-apple crisp for dessert. No oven required! These recipes call for a ceramic smoker, upright barrel smoker, electric smoker, and stovetop smoker. (S1 E09)

programme.title: Somebody's Gotta Do It With Mike Rowe
programme.desc: Before hosting Discovery's "Dirty Jobs," Mike Rowe had a program in San Francisco about folks on a mission -- mad scientists, eccentric collectors, devoted do-gooders -- called "Somebody's Gotta Do It." Now, CNN gives Rowe free rein to scour the country for people who, he says, "do what they do because of a weird mix of love, compulsion, obsession, dedication -- whatever it is. Those people have always interested me." The former series focused on the task, but this version spotlights the personality. Or, as he puts it: "More mission, less poop."

programme.title: Cops Reloaded

This entry in the long-running franchise takes footage from previous episodes and re-edits it to produce new episodes. Some of the program's wildest criminals, most memorable car chases, and craziest moments are featured on "Reloaded."

programme.title: Princess Diana: Unexpected Icon
programme.desc: Diana Spencer was that quiet shy girl who every young lady could relate to. The difference with her is that she became a princess. From then on she shot up into stardom to become one of the biggest icons, not only in the UK, but in the entire world. Directed by Nick Randall.

programme.title: Murderous Minds: Dennis Nilsen
programme.desc: Dennis Andrew Nilsen was a Scottish serial killer and necrophile who murdered at least twelve young men and boys between 1978 and 1983 inLondon. Directed by Remone Jones.

programme.title: Adele: Homecoming
programme.desc: Adele is a Grammy Award-winning artist whose soulful sound has thrust her into the limelight. Her soulful voice, frank personality and chart-topping hits have made her one of the most successful artists of all time. Her authenticity in her work appeals to so many there are no gimmicks with Adele. What you see is what you get. Through her own words, video and live performances, this documentary takes an incredible look at the life and times of one of the greatest artists of all time. Directed by Billy Simpson.

programme.title: Andy Warhol: A Life In Art
programme.desc: From early on in his career, Andy Warhol had an extraordinary ability to find the sacred in the profane. Like so many successful Americans, he was a product of Eastern European immigrant experience, who himself became an icon - a shy, gay, working-class man who became the court painter of the 1960s and 70s, who became one of the most glamorous figure of 20th Century American Art. Directed by Finlay Bald.

programme.title: Banksy: Street Rat
programme.desc: Banksy changed the modern art world, taking it from galleries and onto the street. His identity has always been hidden from the public keeping this secret was key to avoiding any criminal charges. He made headlines for his Kissing Coppers, painted the West Bank Wall and walked into galleries and hung his own work. Banksy is one of the best-known artists of the 21st Century, yet, does anybody know who he really is? Directed by Finlay Bald.

programme.title: Beyonce: Queen B
programme.desc: One of the most recognizable figures in contemporary music. Get lost in the ultimate story of modern day superstardom as we take you through the incredible journey of Beyoncé. Directed by Billy Simpson.

programme.title: Chris Rock: One Man Show
programme.desc: This hilarious, unapologetic, hard-working comic with sharp social punchlines hasnâ€™t always had it easy. In fact he draws his comedy from a darker side of his past. programme.title: On Safari
programme.sub-title: Molori Safari, Madikwe Game Reserve, North West Province
programme.desc: Returning to the magnificent Madikwe Game Reserve for her sixth Safari destination, Sika visits MOLORI SAFARI, a boutique Safari Lodge overlooking the Dwarsberg Mountains and the Madikwe Valley. The Lodge is recognised as one of Southern Africa's most Private and Luxurious Safari Lodges and they are proudly actively engaged in ongoing Conservation efforts. A very unique and special feature is the Whiskey and Gin Bar at the secret Hideout where you can enjoy the magnificence of Wildlife only a few feet away from you around the Lodge's waterhole.

programme.title: Dawn to Dusk
programme.desc: See how Asian creatures survive four extra hours of daylight once a year.

programme.title: On Safari
programme.sub-title: Settlers Drift, Kariega Game Reserve, Eastern Cape Province
programme.desc: Sika's fifth Safari destination is located in an untouched part of the Kariega Game Reserve of the Eastern Cape. SETTLERS DRIFT LODGE is set in a remote valley looking down onto the Bushmans River where last the 1820 Settlers guided their Ox-wagons across the River. It offers a unique and bespoke Luxury Tented Safari experience with abundant Wildlife encounters and even a River Safari.

programme.title: Dawn to Dusk

programme.title: On Safari
programme.sub-title: Kapama Karula, Kapama Private Game Reserve, Greater Kruger
programme.desc: Set on the banks of the perennial Klaserie River, Sika's fourth Safari Destination KAPAMA KARULA is the flagship Five-star lodge of Kapama Game Reserve. KAPAMA, meaning ‘place of peace', is an ultra-luxurious and prestigious Lodge that exudes calmness and depicts the pinnacle of elegant Safari accommodation, with wraparound views of the African bush enhancing the overall sense of tranquillity. A special Safari experience is the Romantic Sleep-out Platform where Guests can sleep under the Stars on a raised platform in the middle of the African plains.

programme.title: Angry Planet | Arctic Winter
programme.desc: George links up with two experienced polar adventurers to explore the frigid Canadian arctic in the depths of winter. The team first uses skis to cross the fractured sea ice of Frobisher Bay, then dog teams to head north through the spectacular Auyuittuq Mountains.

programme.title: Angry Planet | Highway to Hail
programme.desc: George and three side-kicks head straight into the “Bearcage”, looking to get beat up by the biggest, baddest hailstones that Texas and Oklahoma can throw at them. They find it – and their stormchasing vehicles don't look too pretty after coming through these massive storms.

programme.title: Angry Planet | Under Pressure
programme.desc: It's dark, it's cold, there is no air to breath and everything is under extreme pressure. George begins his exploration of the deep with a well-preserved shipwreck in the st.lawrence River, then heads west to dive with undersea explorers Chris Harvey-Clark and Phil Nuyyton, and investigate the newly discovered “Black Smoker” deepsea vents. The show climaxes in Curacao, where we dive to 1000 feet aboard Nuyyton's newest submersible.

programme.title: Angry Planet | 2009 Hurricane

programme.title: Angry Planet | African Hellhole
programme.desc: The Angry Planet team heads to Africa to climb the steep and very active Nyiragongo Volcano in the Congo that erupted in 2002, sending 125,000 people fleeing for their lives. Host/adventurer George Kourounis explores the bubbling lake of molten lava and investigates the risk the dangerous volcano poses to both the people of city of Goma and the Mountain Gorillas who live on the nearby jungle slopes.

programme.title: Angry Planet | The Winds of Autumn

programme.title: Morning Inspiration
programme.desc: Trending Art S1E1 — A curated collection of artwork from both emerging talents and established artists, meticulously handpicked by our expert curation team. The diverse array of artistic visions is a testament to creativity and the power of visual expression.

programme.title: Morning Inspiration
programme.desc: Elegant Objects E1 — Indulge in a mesmerizing collection of artwork that unveils the eternal allure of still life compositions and enchanting objects. Each is a symphony of form, color, and light, celebrating the everyday.

programme.title: Morning Inspiration
programme.desc: Black & White E1 — An extraordinary art collection that embodies timeless beauty and sophistication. Each piece, crafted with a monochromatic palette, reveals the exquisite interplay of light and shadow. From captivating landscapes to evocative portraits, these black and white images evoke a sense of refined elegance, transporting viewers to a realm of tasteful minimalism.

programme.title: Shades of Color
programme.desc: Shades of Color: Shades of Red (S1E3) — Explore a vibrant, passionate art collection where the color red takes center stage. The works showcase red's emotive power, from fiery abstract paintings to crimson-hued photographs, offering a visual journey through the spectrum of human emotions.

programme.title: Shades of Color
programme.desc: Shades Of Color S1E8: Shades of White — Showcasing a myriad of techniques, from delicate brushstrokes to intricate sculptures and photographs, all enveloped in the ethereal essence and clarity of white. These works create a serene atmosphere that immerses the viewer in quiet tranquility.

programme.title: Shades of Color
programme.desc: Shades of Color: Shades Of Gold E13 — A luminous art collection celebrating fortune, warmth, and the rich glow of possibility. Spanning diverse mediums, each piece shimmers with golden hues, while the soundtrack infuses the experience with lively, mystical charm.

programme.title: Shades Of Color: Shades Of Gold
programme.desc: Shades Of Color S1E13: Shades Of Gold — Experience the opulent allure of an art collection adorned with the lustrous elegance of gold. These works illuminate creativity, from shimmering landscapes to gilded abstractions, embodying the richness of this resplendent hue.

programme.title: Shades of Color
programme.desc: Shades of Color S1E2: Shades of Green — Explore a stunning art collection celebrating the color green. From lush landscapes to abstract works, this collection showcases the versatility and serenity of green in a myriad of artistic expressions.
